title: Apache Kafka — LdapLoginModule not blocked in default JAAS denylist
query_goal: Detect cases where the default JAAS login-module denylist does not include LdapLoginModule, allowing an attacker who controls JAAS config to force an outbound LDAP connection to an arbitrary server.

steps.goal: Locate the default denylist policy artifact
steps.description: Bind the constant, field, or literal used as the default value of the disallowed login modules configuration.
steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that binds the default denylist definition.

steps.description: Find the membership test that checks whether the login module name is contained in the disallowed login module set.
steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that binds the policy check call and the checked login module expression.

steps.goal: Relate the policy check to the default denylist artifact
steps.description: Generate a fragment that constrains the denylist set used in the membership check to be derived from the default policy value.

keywords: local data flow from default value to contains qualifier
collection built from split trim collect toSet

steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that proves the check uses the default policy value.

steps.goal: Constrain the default policy to be incomplete
steps.description: Generate a constraint fragment requiring that the default denylist does not contain the required blocked member LdapLoginModule.
steps.expected_output: A where-clause fragment that constrains the policy default to omit the required blocked member.

steps.description: Generate the final query skeleton and select clause that reports the incomplete default denylist at the policy definition site.

reference_query_patterns: select anchor on literal or field

steps.expected_output: A final from/where/select skeleton that reports the violation using the policy default as the alert anchor.
